Security firm Halcyon advised all affected institutions to treat themselves as compromised regardless of ransom outcome and rotate Canvas API keys, OAuth tokens, and SSO credentials immediately.

### Palo Alto Networks Zero-Day
Separately, Palo Alto Networks disclosed CVE-2026-0300, a critical buffer overflow zero-day affecting PA and VM series firewalls, with limited exploitation observed. Patches target May 13 release.

title: China Unveils First National Guidelines to Regulate AI Agent Development and Applications
summary: China's Cyberspace Administration, NDRC, and Ministry of Industry jointly released implementation guidelines defining AI agents as autonomous intelligent systems and outlining 19 application scenarios including geospatial analysis and smart city governance.
contents:
## China Publishes Landmark AI Agent Regulation Framework

### Regulatory Framework
Chinese authorities jointly issued implementation guidelines to promote the standardized application and innovative development of AI agents on May 8, 2026. The Cyberspace Administration of China, National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), and Ministry of Industry and Information Technology (MIIT) collaborated on the document.

### Definition and Scope
The guidelines define AI agents as intelligent systems capable of autonomous perception, memory, decision-making, interaction, and execution. The document recognizes these systems rapidly integrate with cyberspace and the physical world as large language models and other advanced technologies emerge.

### Four Major Areas
The guidelines outline measures across four domains: consolidating development foundations with improved technological infrastructure and standards; ensuring safety and security with risk prevention frameworks; application-driven growth identifying 19 typical scenarios spanning scientific research, industrial development, consumption boost, public welfare, and social governance; and promoting an innovation ecosystem.

### Geospatial and GIS Applications
Among the 19 application scenarios, the guidelines specifically address energy resource management through environmental sensing agents, national land-space resource lifecycle management, urban planning and governance through intelligent agents supporting smart construction, and transportation safety monitoring with enhanced spatial awareness capabilities.

### International Significance
The release stands as China's first dedicated national policy on AI agents, establishing a regulatory framework that many countries have not yet attempted. The guidelines emphasize safety-controllability principles while encouraging rapid innovation and deployment.

title: North Korea Announces Deployment of New 155mm Artillery Capable of Striking Seoul and First Destroyer Commissioning
summary: North Korea revealed plans to deploy new 155mm self-propelled howitzers with 60+ kilometer range to border units and commission its first 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon by mid-June, marking significant military modernization under Kim Jong Un's directive.
contents:
## North Korea Accelerates Military Modernization

### New Artillery Systems
North Korean leader Kim Jong Un visited a munitions factory on May 6 to inspect production of new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzers destined for three battalions along the southern border. The Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) reported the striking range exceeds 60 kilometers, placing Seoul and the greater capital area within direct artillery range from North Korean frontline positions.

### Destroyer Commissioning
Kim Jong Un boarded the 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon on May 7 for a navigation assessment test in the Yellow Sea. He ordered the vessel delivered to the navy by mid-June as scheduled. KCNA photos showed Kim's daughter Ju-ae accompanying him, reinforcing South Korean intelligence assessments about her potential succession role.

### Constitutional Changes
The announcements follow South Korea's revelation that North Korea's newly revised constitution dropped all references to Korean unification, codifying Kim's stance of treating South Korea as a permanent hostile enemy. The amended constitution redefines North Korean territory as only the northern half of the Korean Peninsula.

### Threat Assessment
South Korean analysts at Kyungnam University's Institute for Far Eastern Studies noted the new artillery system demonstrates North Korea's intent to move beyond mass artillery fire toward precision strike capabilities. The Joint Chiefs of Staff confirmed they monitor developments closely under a robust South Korea-US combined defense posture.

### Broader Context
North Korea conducted back-to-back ballistic missile launches in April and tested cluster-bomb warheads and electromagnetic weapons systems, signaling comprehensive military modernization across conventional and strategic forces.

title: IRGC Establishes Persian Gulf Strait Authority with $2M Transit Fees as US Blockade Continues
summary: Iran's IRGC formally launched the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) on May 5, imposing a 40-question vessel declaration form and fees reaching $2 million per ship, establishing institutional control over the Strait of Hormuz independent of any diplomatic deal.
contents:
## IRGC Formalizes Control Over Strait of Hormuz

### New Maritime Authority
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps published new maritime control rules for the Strait of Hormuz and inner Arabian Gulf on May 4-5, establishing the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA). The institution requires every commercial vessel to submit completed declarations via email before entering the waterway, with transit fees reaching $2 million per vessel.

### Constitutional Independence
The PGSA operates under the Supreme Leader's authority via Article 110 of Iran's constitution, placing it beyond President Pezeshkian's control. Even if Iran signs the proposed 14-point memorandum of understanding with the US, the IRGC's transit requirements remain in force until the IRGC itself issues a separate reversal.

### Strategic Timing
The PGSA launched one day after the collapse of Trump's "Project Freedom" escort initiative, which Saudi Arabia effectively ended by denying basing access. With approximately 1,550 ships trapped and Hormuz traffic down 94%, the IRGC filled the vacuum left by the failed American alternative.

### Ongoing Hostilities
US Central Command announced on May 8 that naval forces redirected 57 vessels since the blockade commenced April 13, and disabled two Iranian tankers attempting to enter an Iranian port. Iran's armed forces subsequently engaged US destroyers transiting the strait, launching missiles, drones, and small boats.

### Diplomatic Impasse
Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Esmail Baghaei stated Iran continues reviewing the US proposal while pursuing negotiations in a "new format" through Pakistan. Senior IRGC officials, particularly Commander Ahmad Vahidi, reportedly oppose concessions on strait sovereignty.

title: China Accelerates Energy Superpower Strategy with Record Renewable Capacity Reaching 60% of Total Grid
summary: Xinhua reported China's push to build an energy superpower with renewable energy installations reaching 23.95 billion watts (60.4% of total capacity), as the 15th Five-Year Plan formally enshrines energy independence and clean energy transition goals.
contents:
## China Advances Energy Superpower Ambitions

### Record Renewable Growth
China's National Energy Administration reported that by March 2026, national renewable energy installed capacity reached 23.95 billion watts, growing 22% year-over-year and comprising approximately 60.4% of total grid capacity. First-quarter renewable generation reached 8,829 billion kilowatt-hours, roughly 37.1% of all electricity produced.

### Strategic Framework
The 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) formally enshrines building an "energy superpower" as a national strategic priority. Xi Jinping emphasized that "energy sustenance must remain firmly in our own hands," directing comprehensive reform of the energy structure toward clean, low-carbon, safe, and efficient systems.

### Key Infrastructure Projects
Multiple major projects advance simultaneously: the world's largest 16-megawatt floating offshore wind platform "Three Gorges Navigator" completed installation off Guangdong province; China's first large-scale computing-power-electricity coordination green power direct supply project commenced operations in Ningxia; and renewable energy corridors along the Yangtze River continue expanding capacity.

### Global Energy Context
Amid the ongoing Strait of Hormuz crisis disrupting global oil markets, China's domestic energy strategy gains additional urgency. The country's total electricity consumption exceeded 10 trillion kilowatt-hours historically in 2025, making diversification away from imported fossil fuels a security imperative.

### Technology Leadership
China continues exporting advanced energy technology, including the China-Laos 500kV interconnection project and Central Asia's largest combined-cycle power station in Uzbekistan, demonstrating growing global influence in energy infrastructure.

title: US and Iran Exchange Fire in Strait of Hormuz as Three US Destroyers Transit Waterway
summary: US Central Command reported intercepting unprovoked Iranian attacks including missiles, drones, and small boats as three US Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz, responding with self-defense strikes against Iranian military facilities.
contents:
## US-Iran Military Clash in Strait of Hormuz

### Exchange of Fire
Explosions rang out across the Strait of Hormuz on May 8 as US and Iranian forces exchanged fire in the strategically vital waterway. US Central Command (CENTCOM) reported that Iranian forces launched multiple missiles, drones, and small boats against three US Navy guided-missile destroyers transiting from the Gulf to the Gulf of Oman.

### US Response
CENTCOM stated US forces intercepted all incoming threats and responded with self-defense strikes targeting Iranian military facilities, including launch sites and command centers. Explosions reverberated near Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island, and reportedly near Tehran. President Trump confirmed the destroyers sustained no damage while inflicting "great damage" on Iranian attackers.

### Iranian Account
Iran's top military command alleged US forces first targeted an Iranian oil tanker and another vessel entering the strait. Iran's armed forces "immediately responded" by attacking US military vessels, claiming "significant damage" to American assets.

### Ceasefire Status
Trump characterized the exchange as a "love tap" and insisted the ceasefire remains in effect. He reiterated demands for Iran to sign the proposed deal, stating "there's zero chance" the US allows Iran to obtain nuclear weapons.

### Oil Market Impact
Brent crude rose 2.3% to $102.40 per barrel following the clash, while US-traded crude climbed 2.1% to $96.80. The International Energy Agency has called the Hormuz situation "the biggest energy security threat in history."

### Hungary New PM
Separately, Hungary's Peter Magyar took the oath of office after sweeping Viktor Orban from power in a landslide election, ending 16 years of Fidesz rule.

title: South Korea Accelerates Korean Iron Dome Deployment to 2029 as North Korea Unveils New Seoul-Range Artillery
summary: South Korea advanced its LAMD missile defense deployment by two years to 2029 in response to North Korea's expanding artillery capabilities, including newly revealed 155mm howitzers capable of striking Seoul from border positions.
contents:
## South Korea Fast-Tracks Missile Defense Against North Korean Artillery Threat

### LAMD Acceleration
South Korea's Defense Acquisition Program Administration announced acceleration of the Low Altitude Missile Defense (LAMD) system deployment to 2029, two years ahead of schedule. The decision marks the first time South Korea has planned to deploy prototype guided-missile systems operationally, reflecting urgency about North Korea's expanding rocket and artillery forces.

### North Korean Threat Context
The accelerated timeline follows North Korea's May 8 announcement of new 155mm self-propelled howitzers with ranges exceeding 60 kilometers, sufficient to strike Seoul and surrounding areas from positions north of the Military Demarcation Line. Nearly half of South Korea's 51 million population resides within range of North Korean artillery positions only 40-50 kilometers away.

### System Capabilities
LAMD fills the lowest layer of South Korea's Korea Air and Missile Defense architecture, complementing L-SAM, THAAD, Aegis destroyers, and medium-range systems. Unlike Israel's Iron Dome, the Korean system must withstand sustained military barrages from state-level artillery corps rather than intermittent rocket attacks.

### Development Progress
The programme involves LIG Nex1, Hanwha Aerospace, and Hanwha Systems. The multi-function radar contract totals 131.5 billion won ($34.6 million). Overall programme costs have risen from 650 billion won to 842 billion won ($222 million) due to expanded testing.

### Strategic Assessment
Military planners assess that preserving command-and-control during opening hours of any conflict could determine whether deterrence survives or collapses into uncontrolled escalation. LAMD aims to buy critical time for counter-battery fire and air strike mobilization.

title: US Treasury Sanctions 11 Entities Across China, UAE, and Belarus for Aiding Iran's Weapons Programs
summary: The US Treasury sanctioned 11 entities and 3 individuals in Iran, China, Belarus, and the UAE for helping Iran secure weapons and raw materials for Shahed drone and ballistic missile programs, days before Trump's planned China visit.
contents:
## US Imposes New Sanctions Targeting Iran's Military Industrial Base

### Sanctions Details
The US Treasury announced sanctions against 11 entities and three individuals on May 8 for allegedly aiding Iran's military procurement efforts. The targets span Iran, China, Hong Kong, Belarus, and the United Arab Emirates. Secretary of State Marco Rubio highlighted several China-based entities providing satellite imagery to enable Iran's military strikes against US forces.

### Key Targets
Sanctioned entities include China-based Yushita Shanghai International Trade for weapons procurement facilitation; Dubai-based Elite Energy FZCO for transferring millions to Hong Kong intermediaries; Hong Kong-based HK Hesin Industry and Mustad for procurement intermediary roles; Belarus-based Armory Alliance; and China-based Hitex Insulation Ningbo Co for supplying ballistic missile materials.

### Diplomatic Context
The sanctions arrived days before President Trump's planned trip to China for meetings with President Xi Jinping, and as US-Iran peace negotiations have stalled. Rubio stated the US expects an Iranian response to its latest proposal, while Tehran continues reviewing the terms through Pakistani mediators.

### Broader Pressure Campaign
Treasury indicated readiness to impose secondary sanctions on foreign financial institutions aiding Iran's efforts, including those connected to China's independent "teapot" oil refineries. Brett Erickson of Obsidian Risk Advisors noted the sanctions remain narrowly focused, giving Iran time to adapt procurement routes.

### Nuclear Dimension
The sanctioned procurement networks directly support Iran's drone production capacity (estimated at 10,000 per month) and ballistic missile programs. Trump has insisted Iran "can never have a nuclear weapon," a position that remains the central sticking point in negotiations.

### Iran Blockade Update
US Central Command reported blocking more than 70 tankers from Iranian ports, collectively holding over 166 million barrels worth at least $13 billion.
